text:
Scottie Scheffler and Xander Schauffele have been the best golfers on the PGA Tour by a country mile this year so it’s no surprise they are going head-to-head for the FedExCup in the final event of the season.

They are well clear of the rest of the field and have won three of this year’s four majors, with Schauffele triumphant at theUSPGA and The Open either side of Scheffler winning The Masters and claiming gold at theOlympicsinParis.

Scheffler has won six PGA Tour events with Schauffele, who don’t forget was Olympic champion in Tokyo three years ago, claiming two. It’s amazing to think they put golfers of the highest calibre like Rory McIlroy, Collin Morikawa, Patrick Cantlay and Hideki Matsuyama in the shade.

The post-season format means Scheffler heads into the Tour Championship at East Lake at ten under despite finishing tied 33rd in last week’s BMW Championship at Castle Pines in Colorado.

Schauffele is two shots behind after his T2 at St Jude’s was followed by a T5 last Sunday and his average in the season’s finale at the Atlanta course is an impressive 66.60.

No one else is really in contention, with Keegan Bradley six under after last week’s win and McIlroy having way too much ground to make up two shots further back.

As much as we love and respect Scheffler after some big wins for us in the first four months of the year, Schauffele has to be backed to win his first FedEx title at 12/5 with Sky Bet.

He’s also 5/1 with the same firm, Betfred and 888Sport to win the 72-hole strokeplay at the Tour Championship with Victor Hovland a nice each-way bet to finish in the top eight at 25/1 with bet365.

The same firm have priced Tommy Fleetwood at 30/1 while Aaron Rai is worth an e/w dabble at 100/1 to finish a breakthrough season with a flourish.

• Chelsea hold a 2-0 advantage after the first leg of their Europa Conference League qualifier with Servette at Stamford Bridge last Thursday and they are 13/8 with BoyleSports to celebrate another win by more than one goal in the second leg in Switzerland.

• Stoke, Wrexham, Leyton Orient, Lincoln and AFC Wimbledon form Saturday’s Football League acca.

title: Xander Schauffele can beat Olympic champ Scottie Scheffler in FedExCup | Metro News
description: Scottie Scheffler won Olympic gold in Paris but Xander Schauffele can beat him to FedExCup success if anyone can beat
keywords: Sport,Chelsea FC,English Football League,Europa Conference League,Golf,Metro newspaper,Rory McIlroy,Scottie Scheffler,The Tipster
